prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property: condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor for each atom in formaldehyde; headline number reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index (f⁺) = 0.7083913 (dimensionless) on the carbon atom.
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (exact four-center integrals, no density fitting).
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz.  The Fukui analysis is a set of single-point DFT calculations on this fixed geometry for the neutral (N), cation (N–1), and anion (N+1) states; no geometry optimization was performed (DFT//input-geom).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
• Charge / multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation +1 doublet, anion –1 doublet.
• Software / version: chemkit (PySCF backend, version as in engine; Mulliken charges).
• Convergence & caveats: all three SCF single points converged; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true.  Engine warnings are reproduced verbatim below.
• Live log: the calculation was streamed to its .out file while running; path was displayed at launch and is available on request.  Full result JSON saved to /tmp/formaldehyde_fukui_dft.json.

Key numeric results (dimensionless):
  Atom  Label  Symbol   f⁺        f⁻        f⁰        Dual
     1     1     O    0.46813824  0.46742464  0.46778144  0.00071360
     2     2     C    0.70839130  0.11244422  0.41041776  0.59594709
     3     3     H   –0.08826939  0.21007563  0.06090312 –0.29834503
     4     4     H   –0.08826015  0.21005551  0.06089768 –0.29831566

Most electrophilic site: carbon (atom 2), f⁺ = 0.7083913.
Most nucleophilic site: oxygen (atom 1), f⁻ = 0.46742464.

Warnings (verbatim):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
